title: Matrix3D Object
description: Transient 3D 4x4 matrix. This object is a wrapper over 3D matrix data and is used as way to pass matrix data in and out of the API and as a convenience when operating on matrix data. They are created statically using the create method of the Matrix3D class.

methods.name: asArray
methods.description: Returns the contents of the matrix as a 16 element array.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates an independent copy of this matrix.

methods.name: create
methods.description: Creates a transient 3d matrix object. It is initialized as an identity matrix and is created statically using the Matrix3D.create method.

methods.name: getAsCoordinateSystem
methods.description: Gets the matrix data as the components that define a coordinate system.

methods.name: getCell
methods.description: Gets the value of the specified cell in the 4x4 matrix.

methods.name: isEqualTo
methods.description: Compares this matrix with another matrix and returns True if they're identical.

methods.name: setCell
methods.description: Sets the specified cell in the 4x4 matrix to the specified value.

methods.name: setToAlignCoordinateSystems
methods.description: Sets this matrix to be the matrix that maps from the 'from' coordinate system to the 'to' coordinate system.

methods.name: setToIdentity
methods.description: Resets this matrix to an identify matrix.

methods.name: setToRotateTo
methods.description: Sets to the matrix of rotation that would align the 'from' vector with the 'to' vector. The optional axis argument may be used when the two vectors are perpendicular and in opposite directions to specify a specific solution, but is otherwise ignored

methods.name: setToRotation
methods.description: Sets this matrix to the matrix of rotation by the specified angle, through the specified origin, around the specified axis

methods.name: setWithArray
methods.description: Sets the contents of the array using a 16 element array.

methods.name: setWithCoordinateSystem
methods.description: Sets the matrix based on the components of a coordinate system.

methods.name: transformBy
methods.description: Transforms this matrix using the input matrix.

properties.name: determinant
properties.description: Returns the determinant of the matrix.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: translation
properties.description: Gets and sets the translation component of the matrix.

asArray.title: Matrix3D.asArray Method
asArray.description: Returns the contents of the matrix as a 16 element array.
asArray.return_description: Returns the array of cell values.

classType.title: Matrix3D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Matrix3D.copy Method
copy.description: Creates an independent copy of this matrix.

create.title: Matrix3D.create Method
create.description: Creates a transient 3d matrix object. It is initialized as an identity matrix and is created statically using the Matrix3D.create method.

getAsCoordinateSystem.title: Matrix3D.getAsCoordinateSystem Method
getAsCoordinateSystem.description: Gets the matrix data as the components that define a coordinate system.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The output origin point of the coordinate system.

parameters.name: xAxis
parameters.description: The output x axis direction of the coordinate system.

parameters.name: yAxis
parameters.description: The output y axis direction of the coordinate system.

parameters.name: zAxis
parameters.description: The output z axis direction of the coordinate system.

getCell.title: Matrix3D.getCell Method
getCell.description: Gets the value of the specified cell in the 4x4 matrix.

parameters.name: row
parameters.description: The index of the row. The first row has in index of 0

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: The index of the column. The first column has an index of 0

isEqualTo.title: Matrix3D.isEqualTo Method
isEqualTo.description: Compares this matrix with another matrix and returns True if they're identical.
isEqualTo.return_description: Returns true if the matrices are equal.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: The matrix to compare this matrix to.

setCell.title: Matrix3D.setCell Method
setCell.description: Sets the specified cell in the 4x4 matrix to the specified value.

parameters.name: row
parameters.description: The index of the row. The first row has in index of 0

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: The index of the column. The first column has an index of 0

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The new cell value.

setToAlignCoordinateSystems.title: Matrix3D.setToAlignCoordinateSystems Method
setToAlignCoordinateSystems.description: Sets this matrix to be the matrix that maps from the 'from' coordinate system to the 'to' coordinate system.

syntax.python: returnValue = matrix3D_var.setToAlignCoordinateSystems(fromOrigin, fromXAxis, fromYAxis, fromZAxis, toOrigin, toXAxis, toYAxis, toZAxis)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = matrix3D_var->setToAlignCoordinateSystems(fromOrigin, fromXAxis, fromYAxis, fromZAxis, toOrigin, toXAxis, toYAxis, toZAxis);

parameters.name: fromOrigin
parameters.description: The origin point of the from coordinate system.

parameters.name: fromXAxis
parameters.description: The x axis direction of the from coordinate system.

parameters.name: fromYAxis
parameters.description: The y axis direction of the from coordinate system.

parameters.name: fromZAxis
parameters.description: The z axis direction of the from coordinate system.

parameters.name: toOrigin
parameters.description: The origin point of the to coordinate system.

parameters.name: toXAxis
parameters.description: The x axis direction of the to coordinate system.

parameters.name: toYAxis
parameters.description: The y axis direction of the to coordinate system.

parameters.name: toZAxis
parameters.description: The z axis direction of the to coordinate system.

setToIdentity.title: Matrix3D.setToIdentity Method
setToIdentity.description: Resets this matrix to an identify matrix.

setToRotateTo.title: Matrix3D.setToRotateTo Method
setToRotateTo.description: Sets to the matrix of rotation that would align the 'from' vector with the 'to' vector. The optional axis argument may be used when the two vectors are perpendicular and in opposite directions to specify a specific solution, but is otherwise ignored

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = matrix3D_var.setToRotateTo(from, to)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = matrix3D_var.setToRotateTo(from, to, axis)

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The vector to rotate from.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The vector to rotate to.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The optional axis vector to disambiguate the rotation axis.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

setToRotation.title: Matrix3D.setToRotation Method
setToRotation.description: Sets this matrix to the matrix of rotation by the specified angle, through the specified origin, around the specified axis

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: The rotation angle in radians.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The axis of rotation.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The origin point of the axis of rotation.

related_samples.name: Rendering Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates using the Rendering capabilities in the API. This starts a series of local renderings to generate a series of frames, where the camera is repositioned and a parameter is modified for each frame to create a dynamic animation. To use the sample, have a design open that contains a parameter named "Length". It can be a user or model parameter. The sample will modify this parameter from a value of 0.1 cm to 15 cm, but you can change these values by editing the values of the paramStartVal and paramEndVal variables on lines 90 and 91 of the sample. It expects a folder named "C:\Temp\RenderSample" to exist, and will fail if it doesn't. The rendered frames will be written to that folder.An example rendering is shown below where this file was used. The parameter is modifying a move feature which results in changing the shape of an extrusion. The parameter could be driving anything and you could modify the code to edit more than one parameter. The result of this sample is a folder containing all of the rendered frames. You can process these to create an animation. The sample animation was created using GIMP.

setWithArray.description: Sets the contents of the array using a 16 element array.

parameters.name: cells

transformBy.description: Transforms this matrix using the input matrix.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: The transformation matrix.

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

# Set the value of the property.
